title: luzula pilosa var. saltuensis
intro: Luzula pilosa var. saltuensis, also known as a variant of the hairy woodrush, is a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is commonly found in wetland areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Luzula pilosa var. saltuensis can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
